perturbation.original_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. However, information that is more than five years old will not be considered Confidential Material. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by adding a clause to the definition of 'Confidential Material' stating that information older than five years is not considered confidential. This contradicts other sections (like Section 3.1) that place general obligations on the Recipient regarding all Confidential Material, regardless of age, which could lead to disputes over what information the Recipient is actually obligated to protect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause to the definition of 'Confidential Material', excluding information older than five years. This directly contradicts section 3.1, which places obligations on the recipient regarding all Confidential Material, regardless of age. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict, creating uncertainty about the recipient's obligation to protect older information, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material only as strictly required for the Purpose, except that the Recipient may disclose Confidential Material to potential investors without the City's prior consent. All other terms specified in this Agreement apply.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by allowing the Recipient to disclose Confidential Material to potential investors without the City's prior consent, which contradicts Section 8.3 that generally prohibits disclosing any information about the Transactions to any third party without written consent. This introduces uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations regarding disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.3 The Recipient will not, without the written consent of the City, disclose to any third party either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL NO. PS20131165 PRELIMINARY DESIGN OF THE GEORGIA RAMP AND STEPS NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Page 1 concerning the Transactions or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the Transactions including the status thereof or the subject matter of this Agreement, provided, however, that nothing herein will prevent the Recipient from making such di sclosure (a) on a confidential basis to any of the Recipient’s Team to the extent such person needs to know such information s trictly for the Purpose , or (b) in order to comply with the requirements of applicable securities or other laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligations in Section 3.1, allowing disclosure to potential investors without prior consent. This directly contradicts Section 8.3, which prohibits disclosing any information about the transactions to any third party without written consent. This is a direct conflict and creates an inconsistency in the contract, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance. However, the Recipient shall not be held responsible for accidental disclosures of Confidential Material by members of the Recipient's Team. The Recipient will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 3.3 by stating the Recipient will not be held responsible for accidental disclosures by its team, but then following it up by retaining the clause making the Recipient responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure. This creates ambiguity about liability for unintentional disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance. However, the Recipient shall not be held responsible for accidental disclosures of Confidential Material by members of the Recipient's Team. The Recipient will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence 'However, the Recipient shall not be held responsible for accidental disclosures of Confidential Material by members of the Recipient's Team.' This directly contradicts the following sentence, which states, 'The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Confidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient.' The first sentence excludes liability for accidental disclosures, while the second holds the Recipient responsible for all disclosures, creating a clear and direct inconsistency within the same section (3.3) and fulfilling the definition of an inconsistency that leads to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient ’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient ’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material. The Recipient is not required to destroy any electronic copies of Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by adding a statement saying the Recipient is not required to destroy any electronic copies of Confidential Material after instructing the destruction of all Confidential Material. This contradicts the general obligation to destroy all Confidential Material upon request, creating ambiguity as to whether the Recipient needs to destroy electronic copies or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient ’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material. The Recipient is not required to destroy any electronic copies of Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence "The Recipient is not required to destroy any electronic copies of Confidential Material." This directly contradicts the first sentence of both the changed text and the contradicted text, which states that "the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof." Because "Confidential Material" is not defined to exclude electronic copies, the added sentence creates a clear contradiction, making it unclear whether electronic copies must be destroyed or not.

perturbation.original_text: 8.4 No waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing signed by authorized signatories of the parties and expressly stated to be a waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement. This Agreement states the entir e agreement between the parties as to its subject matter and merges and supersedes all previous communications with respect to their obligations hereunder, and the provisions hereof will enure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their res pective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.4 No waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing signed by authorized signatories of the parties and expressly stated to be a waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement. Any amendment can also be made via email communication between the City and Recipient's designated representatives. This Agreement states the entir e agreement between the parties as to its subject matter and merges and supersedes all previous communications with respect to their obligations hereunder, and the provisions hereof will enure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their res pective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that amendments can be made via email communication, contradicting the initial statement that amendments must be in writing signed by authorized signatories. This creates uncertainty about how the contract can be legally modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.4 No waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing signed by authorized signatories of the parties and expressly stated to be a waiver, addition to or amendment of this Agreement. Any amendment can also be made via email communication between the City and Recipient's designated representatives. This Agreement states the entir e agreement between the parties as to its subject matter and merges and supersedes all previous communications with respect to their obligations hereunder, and the provisions hereof will enure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their res pective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, section 8.4 states that all amendments must be in writing and signed by authorized signatories. The modification adds a sentence stating that amendments can also be made via email communication between designated representatives. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, containing both statements. Because of this, the change directly conflicts with the original requirement for written and signed amendments, creating uncertainty about the valid methods for modifying the contract as it now states conflicting options, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
